cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out the order of these five books on the shelf based on the given clues. Let me start by listing out the books: white, gray, purple, yellow, and black. That means wherever the yellow book is, the white book has to be somewhere to its right. So if I imagine the shelf from left to right, yellow comes before white. Next, the black book is to the left of the yellow book. So black is even further left than yellow. So the order so far is black, then yellow, then white. Then, the purple book is the rightmost. That means purple is the fifth book from the left. So the last position is definitely purple. Also, the white book is to the left of the gray book. So after white comes gray. So putting that together with the previous order: black, yellow, white, gray, and then purple is at the end. Let me try to map this out. Let's denote positions from left to right as positions 1 to 5. We know purple is in position 5. We have the order black < yellow < white < gray. But these four books need to be arranged in positions 1 to 4. Let me see: Black has to be before yellow, yellow before white, and white before gray. So the order is black, then yellow, then white, then gray. So positions 1 to 4 would be black (1), yellow (2), white (3), gray (4), and purple (5). That fits all the given conditions.
